title: hieracium reayense
intro: Hieracium reayense, also known as Reay's hawkweed, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is native to the United Kingdom and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various skin conditions and wounds, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat eczema, acne, and other skin issues, and has also been found to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits. Hieracium reayense can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
